official_tensor.name: official_k_rope_bundle.layer0_k_pre_rope_f32
official_tensor.source_expression: qkv = attn.qkv(normed); k = qkv[:, q_dim:q_dim + kv_dim].contiguous(); layer0_k_pre_rope_f32 = k.reshape(-1).float().cpu().tolist()
official_tensor.serialized_dtype: f32 JSON values converted from torch.bfloat16 tensor values

official_boundary_definition.in_words: Layer-0 K projection output for all prompt tokens after official attn.norm and official attn.qkv Linear, sliced to the K portion, before RoPE/cache write/attention. It is captured before the explicit grouped view call but is contiguous in token-major [token, kv_head, lane] order when reshaped as [74, 8, 64]. The official K-bias slice exists in qkv.bias but is all zeros, so pre-bias and post-bias values are effectively identical for K.
official_boundary_definition.pre_or_post_bias: post-qkv-linear-bias semantically; K bias slice is all zeros so numerically identical to pre-bias
official_boundary_definition.before_or_after_grouped_head_reshape: serialized before the helper view operation; layout is directly reshape-compatible with [token, kv_head, lane]
official_boundary_definition.caveat: The runtime artifact did not expose the full helper-reference vector, so PPP records the runtime-provided comparison metrics rather than recomputing vector diffs locally.

runtime_interpretation.layout: [token, kv_head, lane] contiguous; equivalent flat [token, kv_dim]

runtime_bias_metadata.source: no captured K bias tensor/slice for this exact grouped K path

next_bounded_step: Do not treat official_grouped_pre_rope_k as a different layout or RoPE/bias boundary. Runtime-forward should compare its helper-reference CPU implementation against the official PyTorch attn.qkv K slice semantics/arithmetic for this exact case, or expose the full runtime helper-reference vector so PPP can recompute direct transforms; no attention/logit work yet.
